title: Unique Umami Italian Sauerkraut Ravioli
description: A crowd-pleasing Italian dish featuring sauerkraut and lacquer-glaze, this ravioli is a unique and delicious twist on traditional Roman cuisine. It is also shojin-friendly, making it accessible to a variety of dietary needs.
instructions: Combine sauerkraut, mustard, and nutritional yeast in a bowl and mix well.
In a separate bowl, combine flour, eggs, olive oil, salt, and black pepper to make the pasta dough.
Knead the pasta dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Roll out the pasta dough to a thin sheet, about 1/16 inch thick.
Use a round cookie cutter or a ravioli cutter to cut out circles of pasta.
Place a tablespoon of the sauerkraut mixture in the center of each pasta circle.
Fold the pasta over the filling to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal the ravioli.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the ravioli for 3-5 minutes, or until they float to the surface.
While the ravioli are cooking, heat the lacquer glaze in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Remove the ravioli from the water with a slotted spoon and toss them in the lacquer glaze to coat.
Serve the ravioli hot, garnished with additional sauerkraut and mustard if desired.
